intro: Mummenhoffia oliveri, also known as the Oliver's mustard or white mustard, is a species of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and edible seeds. The plant is believed to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Mummenhoffia oliveri include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
